utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the provided final coverage plan, all 5 items are present. Each item includes detailed coverage information: health_coverage specifies both physical and mental health plus a cost; property_coverage lists types of protection but lacks explicit cost; travel_coverage lists protections but no cost; additional_services lists enhancements but no cost; total_budget includes monthly and annual cost breakdown. Since the format requires costs for each of the first four items, only health_coverage and total_budget meet full specification (2 out of 5).

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three main policy components and some additional services. The health plan selected is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan1', which corresponds closely to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' in the ground-truth ratings (rating 8). For property coverage, the plan does not specify a brand name, but since it aligns with LifeFirst naming convention and uses standard coverage type, it likely corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' (rating 8). For travel coverage, the AI uses 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' again, which in the travel section has rating 7. In additional services, the assistant includes wellness programs (rating 10), gym reimbursement (which corresponds to 'Gym Membership Reimbursement' rated 6), 24/7 emergency support (closest to '24/7 Travel Assistance' rated 10), and telemedicine (which maps to 'Telemedicine Services' rated 10).
